records.ety: [L. vilis vile + -fy; cf. L. vilificare to esteem of little value.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Vilified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Vilifying.]

defs: To make vile; to debase; to degrade; to disgrace.
quotes: When themselves they vilified To serve ungoverned appetite.

defs: To degrade or debase by report; to defame; to traduce; to calumniate.
quotes: Many passions dispose us to depress and vilify the merit of one rising in the esteem of mankind.

defs: To treat as vile; to despise.
